Story Title: CDK Stack Scaffold (Cognito, API Gateway, CloudFront, S3, Lambda roles)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- StatefulStack deploys: Cognito User Pool 'deliverpro-user-pool' with email username per auth-architecture §1
- S3 bucket 'deliverpro-evidence-{accountId}' created with Block Public Access enabled per files-architecture §2
- S3 CORS configured for CloudFront + localhost:5173 per files-architecture §2.1
- StatelessStack deploys: API Gateway REST API with Cognito Authorizer per auth-architecture §3
- CloudFront distribution created with S3 origin and custom error response (403/404 → /index.html 200) per auth-architecture §5
- Lambda execution role with RDS, S3 (PutObject, GetObject), Secrets Manager, SSM read access
- CDK synth succeeds with zero errors
- cdk deploy completes successfully in us-east-1
Notes: Foundation stack for all Phase 2 resources. Must deploy first.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- V003__phase2_additions.sql applies cleanly to existing V002 schema
- macro_checkpoints gains columns: meeting_date DATE, result_detail TEXT, reached_at TIMESTAMPTZ per §4.1
- macro_checkpoints CHECK constraint updated to include 'checklist' type per §4.1
- projects gains column: hours_consumed NUMERIC(8,2) DEFAULT 0 per §4.1
- gate_evidence gains column: link_metadata JSONB per §4.1
- casdm_config gains column: project_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default' per §4.1
- New unique constraint: (phase, item_name, project_type, config_type) per §4.1
- Tables created: weekly_status_logs, escalations, discovery_sessions, onboarding_checklist_items, analysis_prompts per §4.2
- All indexes from §4 created (6 new indexes)
- 3 QuickSight-ready views created: v_project_summary, v_gate_completion, v_timeline per §4.3
- Migration is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS where applicable)

Story Title: Shared Middleware (JWT Validation, RBAC, Error Handler, Request Logger)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- JWT validation middleware verifies Cognito ID token signature using JWKS per auth-architecture §2
- Extracts claims: sub, email, name, cognito:groups from token per auth-architecture §2
- AuthContext type exported with fields: userId, email, name, role, groups per auth-architecture §3
- Role check middleware validates user's group against handler's allowed roles per auth-architecture §3.1
- Returns HTTP 401 with {code: 'UNAUTHORIZED', message: 'Missing or invalid token'} for invalid/expired JWT
- Returns HTTP 403 with {code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Insufficient permissions'} for role mismatch
- Error handler middleware catches AppError and returns structured {code, message, statusCode} per SRS §6 NFR-P2-003
- Request logger logs method, path, userId, duration in structured JSON to CloudWatch
- All middleware exports from packages/shared/middleware/

Story Title: Cognito User Pool + 5 Groups + App Client
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Cognito User Pool 'deliverpro-user-pool' created in us-east-1 per auth-architecture §1
- Password policy: min 8 chars, uppercase + lowercase + number + symbol per §1
- Self-registration disabled per §1
- 5 groups created: admin, leadership, pm, sa, engineer per §1.2
- App client 'deliverpro-spa-client' created with no client secret per §1.3
- OAuth flow: Authorization Code with PKCE per §1.3
- Callback URLs: https://{cloudfront-domain}/callback, http://localhost:5173/callback per §1.3
- Token validity: Access 1hr, ID 1hr, Refresh 30 days per §1.3
- Scopes: openid, email, profile per §1.3
- Cognito domain prefix 'deliverpro-auth' configured per §1.4
Notes: Admin creates users manually. No self-signup.

Story Title: Project CRUD Handlers (list, get, create, update)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/projects returns ProjectListResponse with projects[], next_cursor, total_count per §2.1
- Default filter excludes status='Closed' and 'TEMPLATE' when no status param per §2.1
- Supports query params: status, phase, pm, sa, type, search (ILIKE on title/jira_key), limit (max 100), cursor per §2.1
- current_phase computed at query time per projects-architecture §4 (highest phase with all mandatory checkpoints reached)
- burn_rate_pct computed as (hours_consumed/sow_hours)*100, null if sow_hours is 0 or NULL per §2.1
- GET /api/projects/{projectId} returns full ProjectDetail per §2.2
- Returns 404 {code: 'PROJECT_NOT_FOUND'} for unknown projectId per §2.2
- POST /api/projects creates project with Zod-validated body per §2.3
- Required fields: title, project_type; optional: sow_hours, project_manager, solution_architect, etc per §2.3
- Returns 201 with created project per §2.3
- PATCH /api/projects/{projectId} updates mutable fields per §2.4
- Auth: all handlers require pm, sa, engineer, leadership, or admin role per §2.1
Notes: Core project CRUD. Phase computation is query-time join.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/import-jira triggers one-time Jira CST import per §5.1
- Auth: admin role only per §5.1
- Calls Jira Cloud REST API to fetch all issues from CST board
- Maps Jira fields to projects table: jira_key, title, description, status, project_manager, solution_architect, account_executive, engineers_assigned, planned_kickoff_date, expected_completion_date, sow_hours, project_type per §5.1
- Jira API token read from Secrets Manager per SRS §8.2
- Import is idempotent: SSM parameter '/deliverpro/config/jira-import-completed' set to 'true' after first run per §5.1
- Subsequent attempts return HTTP 409 {code: 'IMPORT_ALREADY_COMPLETE', message: 'Jira import has already been executed'} per FR-P2-009
- Per-issue failures are logged and skipped; response includes success_count and error_count per §5.1
- Returns 200 with {imported: number, errors: number, status: 'completed'}
Notes: One-time import. App replaces Jira going forward.

Story Title: CASDM Template Seeding on Project Create
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- On POST /api/projects (project creation), system seeds micro_artifacts from casdm_config where config_type='micro_artifact' AND project_type matches per §5.2
- Seeds macro_checkpoints from casdm_config where config_type='macro_checkpoint' AND project_type matches per §5.2
- Seeds 9 onboarding_checklist_items per FR-P2-019 AC (Set up Slack/Teams, Set up Clockify, Assign resources, Complete SOW handoff, 5 customer intro emails) per §5.3
- If project_type has no config rows, falls back to project_type='default' per §5.2
- Seeding is transactional — all-or-nothing per §5.2
- Template project (jira_key='__template__') is never returned in list queries per §2.1
Notes: Seeding runs inside the create-project transaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/checklist returns all onboarding_checklist_items for the project per §3.3
- Response includes items[], progress: {completed: number, total: number} per §3.3
- PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/checklist/{itemId} toggles completed field per §3.3
- When completed=true: sets completed_by to current user email, completed_at to now() per §3.3
- When completed=false: clears completed_by and completed_at per §3.3
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin roles per §3.3
- Returns 404 {code: 'CHECKLIST_ITEM_NOT_FOUND'} if itemId invalid per §3.3
- When all 9 items completed, the checklist-type macro_checkpoint for onboarding gets reached_at set per gates-architecture §4

AC:
- PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/hours accepts {hours_consumed: number} per §3.2
- Validates hours_consumed >= 0 via Zod per §3.2
- Updates projects.hours_consumed for the given projectId per §3.2
- Auth: pm, leadership, admin roles per §3.2
- Returns 200 with updated {hours_consumed, sow_hours, burn_rate_pct} per §3.2
- burn_rate_pct = (hours_consumed/sow_hours)*100; null if sow_hours is 0 or NULL per §3.2
- Returns 404 {code: 'PROJECT_NOT_FOUND'} if projectId invalid per §3.2

AC:
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/gates returns GateStatusResponse per §2.1
- Response contains phases[]: each with phase, phase_name, micro_artifacts[], macro_checkpoints[], phase_complete boolean per §2.1
- micro_artifacts include: artifact_name, status, completed_at, completed_by per §2.1
- macro_checkpoints include: checkpoint_name, checkpoint_type, occurred, reviewed_by, meeting_date, reached_at, result_detail, evidence_count per §2.1
- Phase 1 governance_events integrated: reads governance_events table where project_id matches per §5.4
- Governance events interleaved chronologically with checkpoint data per §5.4
- phase_complete = true when all mandatory macro_checkpoints for that phase have reached_at IS NOT NULL per §2.1
- Auto-reconciliation: if governance_events contains a macro gate approval matching a checkpoint, that checkpoint shows reached_at from the event per §5.3
- Returns 404 {code: 'PROJECT_NOT_FOUND'} if projectId invalid per §2.1
- Auth: all authenticated roles per §2.1
Notes: Core governance view. Joins multiple tables.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId} completes a checkpoint per §2.2
- human_review type: accepts {reviewed_by: string}, sets reviewed_by, reviewed_at=now(), reached_at=now() per §4
- meeting type: accepts {occurred: boolean, meeting_date?: string, meeting_link?: string, result_detail?: string}, sets fields + reached_at=now() per §4
- transcript_analysis type: same as meeting (manual completion) per §4; analysis result set separately via FR-P2-005
- checklist type: completion determined by all child items being checked (auto-computed), not manually set per §4
- A checkpoint cannot be un-completed once reached_at is set (append-only) per FR-P2-008
- Returns 409 {code: 'CHECKPOINT_ALREADY_COMPLETE'} if reached_at already set per §4
- Returns 404 {code: 'CHECKPOINT_NOT_FOUND'} if checkpointId invalid per §2.2
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin per §2.2
Notes: 4 checkpoint types with different completion logic.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId}/evidence attaches evidence per §2.3
- Accepts body: {evidence_type: 'meeting_link'|'file_upload'|'url', value: string, label?: string} per §2.3
- Creates gate_evidence row with uploaded_by=current user, created_at=now() per §2.3
- For evidence_type='meeting_link': validates URL matches https://app.avoma.com/* pattern per §6.1
- For evidence_type='file_upload': value must be a valid S3 key (validated format, not existence) per §6.3
- Multiple evidence items can be attached to one checkpoint per FR-P2-003
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId}/evidence lists all evidence per §2.3
- Returns 404 if checkpoint not found per §2.3
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin per §2.3

Story Title: Files Domain — Presigned URL Lambdas (upload + download)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/files/upload-url returns {uploadUrl, s3Key, expiresIn: 300} per §4.1
- Accepts body: {projectId, phase, checkpointName, fileName, contentType} per §4.1
- Validates contentType against allowed MIME types: application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.*, image/png, image/jpeg, text/plain, text/markdown per §4.1
- Validates fileName length <= 255 chars per §4.1
- Returns 400 {code: 'INVALID_CONTENT_TYPE'} for unsupported MIME types per §4.1
- S3 key format: evidence/{projectId}/{phase}/{checkpointName}/{ulid}_{fileName} per §4.1
- POST /api/files/download-url returns {downloadUrl, expiresIn: 300} per §4.2
- Accepts body: {s3Key} per §4.2
- Validates user has access to the project (project-membership check) per §4.2, §7.2
- Returns 403 {code: 'FORBIDDEN'} if user has no access to project per §4.2
- Auth: all authenticated roles for upload; all authenticated roles for download per §4.1, §4.2
Notes: Stateless domain — no DB writes. S3 presigned URLs.

Story Title: Weekly Status Call Log Handlers (create + list)
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/status-logs creates a status log entry per §2.1
- Accepts body: {log_date: string, meeting_link?: string, topics_covered: string, demo_items?: string, blockers?: string} per §2.1
- Validates log_date is not in the future; returns 400 {code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', field: 'log_date', message: 'Date cannot be in the future'} per §2.1
- Validates topics_covered max 4000 chars, demo_items max 2000 chars, blockers max 2000 chars per §2.1
- Sets logged_by to current user email per §2.1
- Returns 201 with created log entry per §2.1
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/status-logs returns all logs sorted by log_date DESC per §2.2
- Supports pagination: limit (default 20, max 50), cursor per §2.2
- Auth: pm, leadership, admin for create; all roles for list per §2.1, §2.2
Notes: Weekly cadence — PM logs each client call.

Story Title: React + Vite + Tailwind Scaffold + Cognito Auth Flow
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Vite + React + TypeScript project initialized in frontend/ directory
- Tailwind CSS configured with design tokens
- Cognito Hosted UI integration: login redirects to Cognito, callback exchanges code for tokens per auth-architecture §1.3
- PKCE flow implemented per §1.3
- Access token stored in memory (not localStorage) per auth-architecture §4
- Refresh token flow: silent refresh before expiry per §4
- Protected route wrapper redirects unauthenticated users to /login
- AuthContext provides: user (email, name, role, groups), isAuthenticated, logout()
- Logout clears tokens and redirects to /login
- Responsive layout shell with sidebar navigation

AC:
- Page renders at /projects route
- Calls GET /api/projects with current filters per projects-architecture §2.1
- Displays project cards with: title, jira_key, current_phase badge, project_type, project_manager, solution_architect, status per FR-P2-001
- Burn rate badge on each card: green (0-70%), yellow (71-90%), red (91%+); hidden if sow_hours null per FR-P2-028
- Search bar filters by title or jira_key (debounced 300ms) per FR-P2-015
- Dropdown filters: status, phase, project_manager, solution_architect, project_type per FR-P2-015
- Filters combine with AND logic per FR-P2-015
- Filter state persists in localStorage per FR-P2-015
- Empty state: 'No projects match your filters' message per FR-P2-015
- Click project card navigates to /projects/{projectId} per FR-P2-001
- Pagination via cursor-based loading (Load More button)

AC:
- Page renders at /projects/{projectId} route
- Calls GET /api/projects/{projectId}/gates per gates-architecture §2.1
- Shows all 5 CASDM phases (0-4) with visual separation per FR-P2-002
- Phase progression indicator: completed phases green, current phase blue, future phases grey per FR-P2-014
- Each phase expandable/collapsible showing micro_artifacts and macro_checkpoints per FR-P2-002
- Micro artifacts show: name, status badge (pending/in_progress/complete), completed_at per FR-P2-002
- Macro checkpoints show: name, type badge, occurred status, reviewed_by, meeting_date, reached_at, evidence count badge per FR-P2-002
- Completed items visually distinct (green checkmark + muted text) per FR-P2-002
- Pending items show action buttons (Mark Complete, Add Evidence) per FR-P2-002
- Loading state shown while fetching; 404 shows 'Project not found'

AC:
- 'Add Evidence' button on each macro checkpoint opens modal per FR-P2-003
- Modal offers 3 options: Paste Meeting Link, Upload File, Enter URL per FR-P2-003
- Meeting Link: validates URL matches https://app.avoma.com/* before submit per gates-architecture §6.1
- File Upload: file picker restricted to PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PNG, JPG, TXT, MD per FR-P2-012
- Client-side validation: reject files > 25 MB with 'File size exceeds 25 MB limit' per FR-P2-012
- Upload flow: calls POST /api/files/upload-url → uploads to S3 via presigned PUT → calls POST /evidence with s3Key per files-architecture §4.1
- Upload progress indicator shown per FR-P2-012
- URL: accepts any valid URL, saves with evidence_type='url' per FR-P2-003
- Evidence list below checkpoint shows: label, type icon, value (clickable), uploaded_by, created_at per FR-P2-003
- Download button calls POST /api/files/download-url and opens presigned GET URL per files-architecture §4.2
Notes: 3-way evidence attachment with S3 direct upload.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Checklist section visible under Phase 0 on project detail page per FR-P2-019
- Displays 9 items as checkboxes with item_name label per FR-P2-019
- Progress indicator shows 'X of 9 complete' per FR-P2-019
- Clicking checkbox calls PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/checklist/{itemId} per projects-architecture §3.3
- Checked items show completed_by and completed_at below the label per FR-P2-019
- Unchecking clears completed_by and completed_at per FR-P2-019
- Optimistic UI update with rollback on error

Story Title: Weekly Status Log UI + Resource Budget Display
Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- Status Logs section on project detail page per FR-P2-020
- 'Log Status Call' button opens form with: log_date (date picker), meeting_link, topics_covered (textarea), demo_items (textarea), blockers (textarea) per FR-P2-020
- Form validates log_date not in future per FR-P2-020
- On submit, calls POST /api/projects/{projectId}/status-logs per meetings-architecture §2.1
- Chronological list shows all logs: date, meeting link (clickable), topics, demo items, blockers, logged_by per FR-P2-020
- Resource Budget section in project detail header per FR-P2-022
- Shows: SOW Hours, Hours Consumed, Burn Rate % with color indicator (green/yellow/red) per FR-P2-022
- 'Update Hours' button opens inline editor for hours_consumed per FR-P2-022
- If sow_hours null/0, shows 'SOW hours not set' per FR-P2-022
Notes: Combined UI for status logs and budget.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/escalations creates an escalation per §2.3
- Accepts body: {raised_date: string, description: string, severity: 'low'|'medium'|'high'|'critical', raised_by: string} per §2.3
- Validates description max 2000 chars, raised_by max 200 chars per §2.3
- Sets status='open', created_at=now() per §2.3
- Returns 201 with created escalation per §2.3
- PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/escalations/{escalationId}/resolve resolves an escalation per §2.4
- Accepts body: {resolved_date: string, resolution_notes: string} per §2.4
- Sets status='resolved', resolved_date, resolution_notes per §2.4
- Returns 409 {code: 'ALREADY_RESOLVED'} if status already 'resolved' per §2.4
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/escalations returns all escalations per §2.5
- Supports filter: severity, status per §2.5
- Sorted by raised_date DESC; open escalations first per §2.5
- Auth: pm, leadership, admin for create/resolve; all roles for list per §2.3

AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/discovery-sessions creates a discovery session per §2.6
- Accepts body: {session_date: string, meeting_link?: string, participants: string, notes?: string} per §2.6
- session_number auto-assigned as MAX(session_number)+1 for project per §2.6
- Validates participants max 1000 chars, notes max 4000 chars per §2.6
- Returns 201 with created session including assigned session_number per §2.6
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/discovery-sessions returns all sessions sorted by session_number ASC per §2.7
- Each session shows: 'Discovery Session [N]', session_date, meeting_link, participants, notes per §2.7
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin for create; all roles for list per §2.6, §2.7

AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId}/notes adds a note per §2.5
- Accepts body: {note_text: string} per §2.5
- Validates note_text max 4000 chars; returns 400 {code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', field: 'note_text', message: 'Note exceeds 4000 character limit'} if exceeded per FR-P2-017
- Sets author=current user email, created_at=now() per §2.5
- Notes are append-only — no edit or delete endpoints per FR-P2-017
- Returns 201 with created note per §2.5
- GET /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId}/notes returns all notes sorted by created_at ASC per §2.6
- Each note shows: note_text, author, created_at per §2.6
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin for create; all roles for list per §2.5

AC:
- Kickoff Prep Meeting checkpoint seeded in Phase 1 with checkpoint_type='meeting' per FR-P2-026
- Executive Check-in Call 1 seeded in Phase 3 with checkpoint_type='meeting' per FR-P2-024
- Executive Check-in Call 2 seeded in Phase 4 with checkpoint_type='meeting' per FR-P2-024
- Account Planning Session seeded in Phase 4 with checkpoint_type='meeting' per FR-P2-027
- All completed via PATCH /api/projects/{projectId}/checkpoints/{checkpointId} with meeting-type payload per §2.2
- result_detail captured for Account Planning ('Revenue opportunities noted' text) per FR-P2-027
- Verify all 4 are included in default CASDM template seed data in casdm_config per §7
- Insert seed rows for these checkpoints in V003 or config seed script
Notes: These checkpoints use existing completion Lambda (DP-11). This story seeds them.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/close initiates closure per §6
- Validates all 4 Phase 4 closure checkpoints are complete per FR-P2-023:
  1. 'Request Signoff from Business Ops' — occurred=true
  2. 'Share Signoff with Customer' — occurred=true
  3. 'Project Closure Meeting/Email' — occurred=true
  4. 'Create Project Closure Deck' — has file_upload evidence attached
- If any incomplete, returns 400 {code: 'CLOSURE_INCOMPLETE', message: 'Not all closure items are complete', missing: string[]} per §6
- If all complete: sets projects.status='Closed' per FR-P2-023
- Returns 200 {status: 'Closed', closed_at: timestamp}
- POST /api/projects/{projectId}/reopen reverses closure (Leadership/Admin only) per FR-P2-023
- Sets status back to 'Active' per §6
- Auth: pm, leadership, admin for close; leadership, admin for reopen per §6

AC:

- On submit calls POST /api/projects/{projectId}/escalations per meetings-architecture §2.3
- Escalation list shows cards sorted by raised_date DESC per FR-P2-021
- Open escalations visually highlighted (red border/badge) per FR-P2-021
- Severity filter dropdown per FR-P2-021
- 'Resolve' button on open escalations opens form: resolved_date, resolution_notes per FR-P2-021
- On resolve calls PATCH /escalations/{id}/resolve per meetings-architecture §2.4
- Resolved escalations show resolution notes and date

AC:

AC:

AC:
- POST /api/analysis/{projectId}/{checkpointId}/fetch-transcript per analysis-architecture §3
- Reads meeting_link from macro_checkpoints for the checkpoint per §3
- Calls Avoma REST API with meeting ID extracted from URL per §3
- Avoma credentials read from Secrets Manager per §4
- On success: stores transcript text in S3 at transcripts/{projectId}/{checkpointName}/{timestamp}.txt per §3
- Updates macro_checkpoints.transcript_url with S3 key per §3
- Returns 200 {transcript_url: string, char_count: number} per §3
- On Avoma API failure: returns 502 {code: 'AVOMA_UNAVAILABLE', message: 'Failed to fetch transcript from Avoma'} per FR-P2-013
- Returns 400 if checkpoint has no meeting_link per §3
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin per §3
Notes: Avoma API integration. Deferred to Iteration 3.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- POST /api/analysis/{projectId}/{checkpointId}/analyze per analysis-architecture §5
- Reads transcript from S3 (transcript_url on checkpoint) per §5
- Reads prompt from analysis_prompts table for the checkpoint_name per §5; falls back to generic prompt if none per FR-P2-005
- Invokes Bedrock AgentCore agent with {transcript + prompt} per §6
- Parses structured response: {topics_covered: string[], topics_missing: string[], passed: boolean, confidence: number} per FR-P2-005
- Stores result in macro_checkpoints.analysis_result (JSONB) per §7
- Sets macro_checkpoints.analysis_run_at = now() per §7
- Creates gate_evidence row with evidence_type='ai_analysis' and JSON result per §7
- Writes result_detail TEXT summary per §7
- Returns 200 with analysis result per §5
- On AgentCore failure: returns 502 {code: 'AGENT_UNAVAILABLE', message: 'Failed to invoke analysis agent'} per FR-P2-005
- Returns 400 if no transcript available per §5
- Auth: pm, sa, leadership, admin per §5
Notes: Core AI analysis flow. AgentCore invocation.

Acceptance Criteria:
AC:
- When evidence is created with evidence_type='meeting_link' or 'url', asynchronous metadata extraction triggered per FR-P2-031
- For Avoma URLs (https://app.avoma.com/*): attempt to extract title, date, duration from Avoma API per FR-P2-031
- For Teams URLs (https://teams.microsoft.com/*): attempt to extract title and date from URL params per FR-P2-031
- For SharePoint URLs (https://*.sharepoint.com/*): attempt to extract document title per FR-P2-031
- Extracted metadata stored as JSONB in gate_evidence.link_metadata per unified-data-model §4.1
- Schema: {title: string, date: string, duration_minutes: number | null} per FR-P2-031
- If extraction fails (network error, unsupported format): link_metadata remains NULL — no error shown per FR-P2-031
- Extraction is non-blocking: evidence record created immediately, metadata populated async per FR-P2-031
- UI displays extracted metadata (title, date, duration) below evidence link when available per FR-P2-031
